title: acacia excelsa subsp. excelsa
intro: Acacia excelsa subsp. excelsa, also known as the Ficus-like Acacia or Philippine Dark Acacia, is a species of tree native to the Philippines and parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. Consuming Acacia excelsa subsp. excelsa has been associated with several health benefits, such as improved cardiovascular health, enhanced immune system function, and even potential anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of Acacia excelsa subsp. excelsa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
